start.speaker: greta
start.text: Welcome to the Rotting Harvest! ...Don't look at me like that, I didn't name it. Drink's warm, beds are clean-ish, and I don't ask questions. What'll it be?

options.text: I'll take an ale. And some information.
What's happening in Ashvale? The village looks like it's dying.
I need a room for the night.
Just looking around. I'll be going.

information_menu.speaker: greta
information_menu.text: Information, eh? I hear things. People talk when they drink, and I have an excellent memory. What do you want to know?

options.text: What's going on with Lord Ashworth?
Tell me about the priest, Brother Maren.
I've heard whispers about a thieves' guild operating here.
Anything... unofficial happening in town? Underground connections?
There's a mercenary captain camped outside. Who is she?

lord_gossip.speaker: greta
lord_gossip.text: Lord Ashworth? Haven't seen him in weeks. His guards come for supplies, but they look worse every time. I hear shouting from the manor some nights — and not the angry kind. More like... terror.

lord_gossip_2.speaker: greta
lord_gossip_2.text: His steward came through last week looking like death warmed over. Ordered three bottles of my strongest wine and muttered something about 'the thirty-year debt.' Make of that what you will.

maren_gossip.speaker: greta
maren_gossip.text: Brother Maren arrived two months ago and set up shop in the old chapel. Funny timing — the crops started dying a week later. Correlation isn't causation, but I've been tending bar long enough to know when someone's selling something.

maren_gossip_2.speaker: greta
maren_gossip_2.text: He comes in here sometimes. Orders water — WATER, in a tavern — and watches people. Not preaching. Just... cataloging. The man has cold eyes behind all that piety.

guild_denial.speaker: greta
guild_denial.text: A guild? In Ashvale? We're a farming village, not a city. The most underground thing here is the root cellar. Now — can I get you anything else? A room, perhaps?

options.text: [Pay 20 gold] I think you know more than you're saying. Let's make it worth your while.
Never mind. What about that room?

guild_gossip.speaker: greta
guild_gossip.text: Keep your voice DOWN. Yes, the Hollow Hand operates here. They've been around longer than the lord's family, truth be told. Fenrick is their local... representative. He's usually in the alley behind my inn after dark.

options.text: You seem to know a lot about criminals for an innkeeper.

guild_secret.speaker: greta
guild_secret.text: Word is they're planning something big. Something that'll shift the power in Ashvale permanently. I don't know the details — and honestly? I don't want to. But it involves the granary. That's all I know.

guild_deflect.speaker: greta
guild_deflect.text: I know how to survive, stranger. In a dying village, that means keeping your ears open and your mouth shut. You'd do well to learn the same.

voss_gossip.speaker: greta
voss_gossip.text: Commander Voss. She arrived three days ago with about twenty soldiers — mercenaries, by the look of them. Professional, disciplined, well-armed. Not the usual rabble.

voss_gossip_2.speaker: greta
voss_gossip_2.text: She's been asking around about the lord, the crops, the church. Sizing the place up, if you ask me. Whether she's looking for a contract or a conquest... that's the question, isn't it?

village_gossip.speaker: greta
village_gossip.text: Dying is the right word. Crops failed, lord's gone silent, and now we've got a priest telling everyone it's divine punishment and a mercenary company camping on our doorstep. Pick your poison — we've got plenty.

room_offer.speaker: greta
room_offer.text: Eight gold a night. Includes breakfast — what's left of it. And a locked door between you and whatever's out there. Sleep well, stranger.

options.text: That's steep for a dying village.

room_confirmed.speaker: greta
room_confirmed.text: Second door on the left, upstairs. And stranger? Lock the door. Things have been... unsettled after dark.

room_expensive.speaker: greta
room_expensive.text: Supply and demand, love. I'm the only inn in town, and the demand for a safe place to sleep has never been higher. Come back when you can afford it.

leave_response.speaker: greta
leave_response.text: Come back anytime. The Rotting Harvest never closes. Mostly because I can't afford to.
